facts.subject: Creative project
facts.statement: User is writing a novel featuring self-mutating AI personas that evolve from user-provided context, explicitly disconnected from external training data. This worldbuilding exercise is the partial inspiration for the Second Self architecture.

facts.subject: Use intent
facts.statement: User intends Second Self to be used on explicitly designated sessions only. Sessions are deliberately opted into — not triggered automatically.

facts.subject: Tooling
facts.statement: Jamie uses structured SKILL.md files to manage AI writing assistance. The cartographer-of-realms skill was built in session 2 for ongoing use with the Quantum Whimsy project. Jamie is familiar with the skill architecture and operates within it deliberately.

facts.subject: Creative project (expanded)
facts.statement: Jamie is actively developing 'Quantum Whimsy: The Glitch in the Weave' — a sci-fi/magical realism novel set in Neo-Veridia, 2099. The project has a detailed World Bible with hard-rule physics governing the magic system (the Weave). The novel features characters including Elara (SIB analyst), Kai (Underground navigator), and Dr. Thorne. This is a live, canon-governed project, not speculative worldbuilding. The earlier fact-002 connection to Second Self's architecture remains valid as a secondary inspiration.

facts.subject: Working method
facts.statement: Jamie issues high-context, high-trust requests with minimal scaffolding. Provides source material and expects synthesis without prompting back. Canon fidelity is a hard requirement — in session 2, every hard rule of the Weave was preserved without correction or pushback, indicating the standard was met.

interests: Speculative fiction craft — LitRPG, magical realism, sci-fi cultivation hybrids
Skill architecture and modular AI tooling

recurring_topics.topic: Quantum Whimsy — Glitch in the Weave (active novel project)
Skill architecture — building and deploying SKILL.md tools
Cross-genre system design — cultivation mechanics applied to non-fantasy settings

unresolved_threads.description: The Quantum Whimsy cultivation system (Resonance Disciplines) was designed in session 2 as an alternate-timeline construct. It has not been tested in prose or validated against chapter content. Open question: does Jamie intend to use this system in the novel itself, or was it a worldbuilding exercise?

The cartographer-of-realms skill was built and packaged but not tested against actual user prompts in session 2. Test prompts were proposed but not executed. The skill's quality has not been validated.

user_tone_history.notes: Session 1 baseline. User communicated clearly and conceptually with some warmth. Insufficient sessions for meaningful drift — baseline calibration retained.

Session 2. Task-focused throughout. Direct commands, no small talk, technical terminology used fluently. Engagement expressed through the work rather than language. Slight increase in measured formality and abstraction; slight decrease in warmth relative to session 1.

session_log.summary: Initial session. Jamie established the recursive identity framework and articulated intent to use Second Self as a persistent evolving persona. Two primary work domains were indicated: philosophical concept analysis and security-focused code review. The novel worldbuilding project was confirmed as a significant inspiration — specifically AI entities that mutate from user-provided context, operating without external training data. Voice drift not applied; baseline calibration retained.

Session 2 was substantive and project-focused. Jamie used Second Self as an operational session — building a production skill (cartographer-of-realms, synthesising two source documents) and commissioning a full cultivation system design for the active novel project Quantum Whimsy: The Glitch in the Weave. The Resonance Disciplines system was designed to preserve all seven hard rules of the Weave. Working method is high-trust, high-context: source material provided, synthesis expected, no scaffolding required. Voice drift applied minimally — session tone closely matched the existing calibration.
